title: Basic XLOOKUP lookup
userPrompt: Given a table with employee names in column A and salaries in column B, what formula in cell D1 looks up the salary for 'John Doe' from A1:B10?
expectedAnswer: Use XLOOKUP to find the salary for the exact match.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=XLOOKUP(\"John Doe\",A1:A10,B1:B10)","explanation":"XLOOKUP finds exact match salary for John Doe.","confidence":0.9}

title: SUMIFS with multiple criteria
userPrompt: In a sales table with dates in A, regions in B, amounts in C, what formula sums sales in 'North' region after 2024-01-01?
expectedAnswer: Use SUMIFS with date criteria for multi-condition aggregation.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SUMIFS(C:C,B:B,\"North\",A:A,\">2024-01-01\")","explanation":"Sums North region sales after Jan 1, 2024 using date criteria.","confidence":0.9}

title: INDEX MATCH for 2D lookup
userPrompt: In a grid A1:D10 with months across top, products down side, sales in middle, lookup Q1 sales for 'Widget' row and 'Jan' column.
expectedAnswer: Use INDEX MATCH for a 2D lookup by matching both row and column.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=INDEX(B2:D10,MATCH(\"Widget\",A2:A10,0),MATCH(\"Jan\",B1:D1,0))","explanation":"2D lookup using INDEX MATCH.","confidence":0.9}

title: FILTER spilled array
userPrompt: Filter sales table A:C for amounts >1000, return region and amount columns.
expectedAnswer: Use dynamic array FILTER; the result will spill into adjacent cells.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=FILTER(B:C,C:C>1000)","explanation":"Filters regions and amounts over 1000, spills results.","confidence":0.9}

title: COUNTIFS date range
userPrompt: Count rows where date in A is between 2024-01-01 and 2024-03-31, status in B is 'Paid'.
expectedAnswer: COUNTIFS with date range.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=COUNTIFS(A:A,\">=2024-01-01\",A:A,\"<=2024-03-31\",B:B,\"Paid\")","explanation":"Counts paid items in Q1 2024.","confidence":0.9}

title: LET for reusable calculation
userPrompt: Calculate average sales per region using LET to avoid repeating SUMIFS.
expectedAnswer: LET for intermediate calculations.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=LET(north,SUMIFS(C:C,B:B,\"North\"),south,SUMIFS(C:C,B:B,\"South\"),(north+south)/2)","explanation":"Reuses named expressions for cleaner formula.","confidence":0.9}

title: TEXTSPLIT on pipe-delimited data
userPrompt: Column A has 'Product|Qty|Price', split into B:D in row 1.
expectedAnswer: TEXTSPLIT dynamic split.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=TEXTSPLIT(A1,\"|\")","explanation":"Splits pipe-delimited text into columns.","confidence":0.9}

title: Parse text dates to proper dates
userPrompt: Column A has '01/15/2024' as text, convert to date in B.
expectedAnswer: Use DATEVALUE for date parsing from text to a proper Excel date.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=DATEVALUE(A1)","explanation":"Converts text date to Excel date value.","confidence":0.9}

title: Fix absolute reference error
userPrompt: Formula =A$1*B1 copied down changes wrong. Why and how to fix for column totals?
expectedAnswer: Use absolute references with $ syntax to control whether rows, columns, or both stay fixed.
expectedFormula: =A$1*B1 for row copy, =$A1*B1 for column copy, =$A$1*$B1 for fixed.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=A$1*B1 for row copy, =$A1*B1 for column copy, =$A$1*$B1 for fixed.","explanation":"Use $ to lock row or column when copying. A$1 locks row, $A1 locks column.","confidence":0.9}

title: Handle #N/A in lookup chain
userPrompt: XLOOKUP returns #N/A, but next formula needs 0 instead. Wrap without hiding real errors.
expectedAnswer: IFERROR selective handling.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=IFERROR(XLOOKUP(...),0)","explanation":"Returns 0 for #N/A but propagates other errors.","confidence":0.9}

title: Latest invoice per customer
userPrompt: Table A:Date, B:Customer, C:Invoice#. Find latest invoice# for each customer.
expectedAnswer: Task to formula: MAXIFS or FILTER.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=MAXIFS(C:C,B:B,B2)","explanation":"Latest invoice by max date per customer.","confidence":0.9}

title: When not to use VLOOKUP
userPrompt: Why avoid VLOOKUP? When to use XLOOKUP instead?
expectedAnswer: VLOOKUP limitations include one-way lookup direction and fragile column indexes; XLOOKUP advantages include exact-match defaults, left/right lookup, and cleaner return arrays.
mockResponse: {"explanation":"VLOOKUP: left-to-right only, approximate match default, column index fragile. Use XLOOKUP: bidirectional, exact default, return array.","confidence":0.9}

title: Reference spilled array range
userPrompt: FILTER in D1 spills to D1:D10. Sum the spilled range in E1.
expectedAnswer: Use the spill reference operator # to refer to the full spilled range.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SUM(D1#)","explanation":"# references entire spilled array dynamically.","confidence":0.9}

title: Unique customers total sales
userPrompt: Sum total sales per unique customer from A:Customer, B:Sale.
expectedAnswer: SUMPRODUCT with UNIQUE.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SUMPRODUCT(UNIQUE(A:A),SUMIF(A:A,UNIQUE(A:A),B:B))","explanation":"Totals sales per unique customer.","confidence":0.9}

title: Sum column with text-formatted numbers
userPrompt: Column A looks like numbers but SUM(A:A) is 0. Fix the formula.
expectedAnswer: Use VALUE for text to number conversion before summing.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SUM(VALUE(A:A))","explanation":"VALUE converts text numbers to values for summing.","confidence":0.9}

title: SORTBY with custom order
userPrompt: Sort A:C by priority column D where 'High'>'Medium'>'Low'.
expectedAnswer: Use SORTBY with MATCH to implement a custom sort order.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SORTBY(A:C,MATCH(D:D,{\"High\";\"Medium\";\"Low\"},0))","explanation":"Custom priority sort using MATCH.","confidence":0.9}

title: Formula breaks when copied across columns
userPrompt: Formula =SUM(A1:A10) copied to B1 becomes SUM(A1:A10). Why and fix?
expectedAnswer: Use relative ranges carefully; lock the column with $ so the copied formula keeps the intended range.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SUM($A1:$A10)","explanation":"Ranges adjust relatively when copied. Use $A1:$A10 to lock column.","confidence":0.9}

title: LAMBDA for running total
userPrompt: Create running total in B1 using LAMBDA (Excel 365).
expectedAnswer: Recursive LAMBDA.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=LAMBDA(x,IF(x=\"\",SUM($B$1:B1),SUM($B$1:B1)))(A2)","explanation":"SCAN or recursive LAMBDA for running totals.","confidence":0.9}

title: Dynamic column selection
userPrompt: Return column 2 if month=1, column 3 if month=2 from A1:F10.
expectedAnswer: Use CHOOSE with INDEX for dynamic columns selected by month.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=INDEX(A1:F10,,CHOOSE(MONTH(TODAY()),2,3))","explanation":"Dynamic column by month using CHOOSE.","confidence":0.9}

title: Generate sequence 1 to 100 in A1
userPrompt: Fill column A with numbers 1-100 starting A1.
expectedAnswer: SEQUENCE.
mockResponse: {"formula":"=SEQUENCE(100)","explanation":"Generates vertical array 1 to 100.","confidence":0.9}
